verses.text: David sings this song after God delivered him from all his enemies, piling up names for God: rock, fortress, deliverer, shield, high tower, refuge. One word is not enough for such a Savior. A heart full of rescue reaches for image after image to say what God has been.

David sings this song after God delivered him from all his enemies, piling up names for God: rock, fortress, deliverer, shield, high tower, refuge. One word is not enough for such a Savior. A heart full of rescue reaches for image after image to say what God has been.

David sings this song after God delivered him from all his enemies, piling up names for God: rock, fortress, deliverer, shield, high tower, refuge. One word is not enough for such a Savior. A heart full of rescue reaches for image after image to say what God has been.

David sings this song after God delivered him from all his enemies, piling up names for God: rock, fortress, deliverer, shield, high tower, refuge. One word is not enough for such a Savior. A heart full of rescue reaches for image after image to say what God has been.

He remembers the terror — 'the waves of death,' 'the snares of hell' — and his one recourse: 'in my distress I called upon the LORD.' From His temple God heard his cry. Prayer is the drowning man's rope; when death's floods close in, the call of faith reaches heaven.

He remembers the terror — 'the waves of death,' 'the snares of hell' — and his one recourse: 'in my distress I called upon the LORD.' From His temple God heard his cry. Prayer is the drowning man's rope; when death's floods close in, the call of faith reaches heaven.

He remembers the terror — 'the waves of death,' 'the snares of hell' — and his one recourse: 'in my distress I called upon the LORD.' From His temple God heard his cry. Prayer is the drowning man's rope; when death's floods close in, the call of faith reaches heaven.

David pictures God's rescue as an earthquake: the earth shakes, the heavens bow, He comes down with darkness under His feet. The Almighty is not indifferent to His servant's cry. When God moves to save, all creation feels the tread of His coming.

David pictures God's rescue as an earthquake: the earth shakes, the heavens bow, He comes down with darkness under His feet. The Almighty is not indifferent to His servant's cry. When God moves to save, all creation feels the tread of His coming.

David pictures God's rescue as an earthquake: the earth shakes, the heavens bow, He comes down with darkness under His feet. The Almighty is not indifferent to His servant's cry. When God moves to save, all creation feels the tread of His coming.

He rides upon a cherub, flies on the wings of the wind, wraps Himself in dark waters and thick clouds, brightness blazing before Him. The God who saves is awesome beyond picturing. Such majesty bends down to rescue one man — that is the wonder of His grace.

He rides upon a cherub, flies on the wings of the wind, wraps Himself in dark waters and thick clouds, brightness blazing before Him. The God who saves is awesome beyond picturing. Such majesty bends down to rescue one man — that is the wonder of His grace.

He rides upon a cherub, flies on the wings of the wind, wraps Himself in dark waters and thick clouds, brightness blazing before Him. The God who saves is awesome beyond picturing. Such majesty bends down to rescue one man — that is the wonder of His grace.

The LORD thunders from heaven, shoots lightnings like arrows, and at His blast the very channels of the sea are laid bare. No enemy can stand before such power. The God who commands the storm fights for His own; His rebuke scatters what no human strength could face.

The LORD thunders from heaven, shoots lightnings like arrows, and at His blast the very channels of the sea are laid bare. No enemy can stand before such power. The God who commands the storm fights for His own; His rebuke scatters what no human strength could face.

'He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.' And the reason astonishes: 'he delivered me, because he delighted in me.' God rescues not because we deserve it but because He loves us. We are saved by His delight, not by our merit.

'He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.' And the reason astonishes: 'he delivered me, because he delighted in me.' God rescues not because we deserve it but because He loves us. We are saved by His delight, not by our merit.

'He sent from above, he took me, he drew me out of many waters.' And the reason astonishes: 'he delivered me, because he delighted in me.' God rescues not because we deserve it but because He loves us. We are saved by His delight, not by our merit.

David speaks of being rewarded 'according to my righteousness' and clean hands. Not sinless perfection, but the sincere, God-given integrity of a heart that kept the LORD's ways. God notices and honours the upright walk of those who truly seek to please Him.

'With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful... with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury.' God meets us in the spirit we bring. He will save the afflicted but bring down the proud. The humble find Him gentle; the haughty find Him their opponent.

'With the merciful thou wilt shew thyself merciful... with the froward thou wilt shew thyself unsavoury.' God meets us in the spirit we bring. He will save the afflicted but bring down the proud. The humble find Him gentle; the haughty find Him their opponent.

'Thou art my lamp, O LORD; the LORD will lighten my darkness.' By God's strength David ran through troops and leaped walls. 'As for God, his way is perfect... he is a buckler to all that trust in him.' His way never fails; His shield covers all who hide in Him.

'Thou art my lamp, O LORD; the LORD will lighten my darkness.' By God's strength David ran through troops and leaped walls. 'As for God, his way is perfect... he is a buckler to all that trust in him.' His way never fails; His shield covers all who hide in Him.

'Thou art my lamp, O LORD; the LORD will lighten my darkness.' By God's strength David ran through troops and leaped walls. 'As for God, his way is perfect... he is a buckler to all that trust in him.' His way never fails; His shield covers all who hide in Him.

'Who is God, save the LORD?' He alone girds David with strength and makes his way perfect, his feet sure as a deer's on the heights. There is no other rock. The poise to stand on dangerous heights is not native skill but the gift of the God who steadies us.

'Who is God, save the LORD?' He alone girds David with strength and makes his way perfect, his feet sure as a deer's on the heights. There is no other rock. The poise to stand on dangerous heights is not native skill but the gift of the God who steadies us.

God trains his hands for battle, gives the shield of salvation, and 'thy gentleness hath made me great.' He enlarges David's steps so he does not slip. Strangely, it is God's gentleness, not His severity, that lifts us — His patient kindness makes small servants great.

David tells of pursuing and crushing his enemies, who could not rise, for 'thou hast girded me with strength to battle.' The victory is God's gift, not his own arm. Whatever foe we overcome, it is the LORD who armed us for the fight and gave the win.

His enemies turned their backs and cried out, even to the LORD, but found no answer; David beat them small as the dust. There is a point where the rebellious cry too late. Better to seek God in the day of grace than to call only when judgment has closed in.

God delivered David from strife and made him head of nations; strangers submit and come trembling out of their strongholds. The shepherd boy now rules peoples he never knew. God lifts the lowly to heights they never imagined, for His own purposes among the nations.

The song ends in praise: 'The LORD liveth; and blessed be my rock.' He is 'the tower of salvation for his king,' showing mercy 'to David, and to his seed for evermore.' The covenant reaches past David to Christ, his seed forever. The living God is praised as the rescuer of His anointed.
